heading: Petition and review
label: 6306 Petition and review
notes: References in TextThe Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, referred to in subsec. (b)(2), are set out in the Appendix to Title 28, Judiciary and Judicial Procedure.

children.label: A
children.text: stating that the order, any provision of the order, or any obligation imposed in connection with the order is not established in accordance with law; and

children.label: B
children.text: requesting a modification of the order or an exemption from the order.

children.heading: Hearings
children.label: 2 Hearings
children.text: The petitioner shall be given the opportunity for a hearing on a petition filed under paragraph (1), in accordance with regulations issued by the Secretary.

children.heading: Ruling
children.label: 3 Ruling
children.text: After a hearing under paragraph (2), the Secretary shall make a ruling on the petition that is the subject of the hearing, which shall be final if such ruling is in accordance with applicable law.

children.heading: Commencement of action
children.label: 1 Commencement of action
children.text: The district court of the United States in any district in which the person who is a petitioner under subsection (a) resides or carries on business shall have jurisdiction to review a ruling on the petition of such person under such subsection, if a complaint for that purpose is filed not later than 20 days after the date of the entry of a ruling by the Secretary under such subsection (a).

children.heading: Process
children.label: 2 Process
children.text: Service of process in a proceeding under paragraph (1) shall be conducted in accordance with the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure.

children.label: A
children.text: to make such ruling as the court shall determine to be in accordance with law; or

children.label: B
children.text: to take such further proceedings as, in the opinion of the court, the law requires.

children.heading: Enforcement
children.label: 4 Enforcement
children.text: The pendency of proceedings instituted under subsection (a) shall not impede, hinder, or delay the Attorney General or the Secretary from taking any action under .section 6307 of this title
